evidence: twilio.ts:563-574 validateTwilioStart throws on sampleRate mismatch: `const sampleRate = numberFromString(format.sampleRate); if (sampleRate !== expectedSampleRateHz) throw new Error(...)`. BUT expectedSampleRateHz is NOT hardcoded 8000 — it is the configurable option `twilioSampleRateHz` (twilio.ts:45 `readonly twilioSampleRateHz?: number;`, twilio.ts:129 `const twilioSampleRateHz = positiveInteger(options.twilioSampleRateHz) ?? DEFAULT_TWILIO_SAMPLE_RATE_HZ;`, passed at twilio.ts:291 `validateTwilioStart(start, twilioSampleRateHz)`). Kimi's own suggested fix ('make expectedSampleRateHz configurable') is ALREADY implemented. Separately, the carrier-upgrade premise is wrong: the inbound media path is unconditionally G.711 mu-law decode (twilio.ts:305 `const pcm8k = decodeMuLawToPcm16(ulaw);`) with no codec/rate branch; Twilio Media Streams only ever streams PCMU/mu-law at 8000 Hz — mediaFormat.sampleRate is fixed at 8000 by the <Stream> protocol, not negotiated per-trunk by the carrier. Accepting a declared 16000 here would not help: the bytes are still mu-law and `decodeMuLawToPcm16` would mislabel them, breaking resampling at twilio.ts:307. The Telnyx/SmartPBX handlers DO have real codec branches (telnyx.ts:661 PCMU, smartpbx.ts:445 g711_ulaw), but Twilio's protocol has no such variation.
real_fix: No code change required for correctness. The strict guard is intentional input validation against a fixed-protocol transport (Twilio Media Streams = PCMU 8000 Hz mono only), and the expected rate is already operator-configurable via the `twilioSampleRateHz` constructor option for any non-standard deployment. If a clearer operator signal is wanted, the only worthwhile tweak is the error message at twilio.ts:572 to state the supported value (e.g. `Unsupported Twilio sample rate ${format.sampleRate}; expected ${expectedSampleRateHz} (Twilio Media Streams = G.711 µ-law 8 kHz)`). Resampling on a declared 16000 (kimi's other suggestion) would be actively wrong because the payload codec is mu-law regardless of the declared rate.
